thesis_hint: Explore a new concept this cycle. Recent journal history is still over-concentrated in 1h work and in reclaim/snapback-style structures, while the only clear walk-forward pass (ETH channel shortbias v3) sits inside that overused 1h lane and should not be over-farmed this turn. Rotate to IO 4h and test a negative-funding base-break retest continuation: require funding to stay materially negative, price to break out of a multi-bar base, then hold a retest above the broken base with expanding directional strength and a rising medium trend anchor. This is not a washout reclaim, not a snapback fade, and not a channel-failure inversion; the question is whether a mildly crowded 4h alt rewards breakout retest continuation better than reversal logic.
stop_condition: Stop when any variant reaches QScore >= 1.0 with profit_factor >= 1.12, total_trades >= 18, and max_drawdown_pct <= 9.0; abort any variant with total_trades < 12, profit_factor < 1.02, or trending regime profit_factor < 1.00.
rotation_reason: Force exploration: 1h still dominates the recent journal, reclaim/snapback concepts remain overrepresented, and rotation rules require a category change away from the overused exploit lane even though ETH channel now has a valid walk-forward pass.
